skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: Which quadratic equation has no real solutions?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The discriminant b² − 4ac determines the number of real solutions. For choice B (3, −12, 36): (−12)² − 4(3)(36) = 144 − 432 = −288 < 0, so no real solutions. Choice A has discriminant 0 (one solution). Choices C and D have positive discriminants (two real solutions).
